Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate reader, publisher, inclusive, privacy, and changed-case product outcomes; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define changed-format, malformed XML, stale response, async reorder, tenant, cancellation, restore, and unfamiliar-reader cases.
